behaviors.description: Plugin with missing required manifest field is rejected before its code executes
behaviors.justification: A Unit test can verify the validation logic rejects the manifest, but cannot prove that the plugin's entry code never actually runs. Real failure mode: invalid plugins are silently loaded or partially execute. To catch this, must create a real plugin file with side effects (marker file, global state) and verify those side effects do not occur when manifest validation fails. Mocking require/import would hide execution bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real plugin directory with manifest.json missing a required field (e.g., 'name'). Create an entry.js that writes a marker file on load. Run the loader and assert: (1) loader rejects the plugin with an error, (2) marker file does NOT exist. Repeat to ensure consistency. Failure case: if code executes despite invalid manifest, marker file appears.

behaviors.description: When plugin A depends on plugin B, B's onLoad hook executes before A's onLoad
behaviors.justification: A Unit test can mock the dependency graph and verify the ordering algorithm produces [B, A], but cannot verify real hook execution order in practice. Real failure modes: async race conditions, module loading order bugs, or misalignment between graph logic and actual hook invocation. Only real plugin files with real onLoad exports and actual module loading can expose these. Mocking hooks would pass even if real execution order is wrong.
behaviors.test_description: Create two real plugins with manifest.json: PluginB (no dependencies) and PluginA (depends on PluginB). Each has an onLoad hook that appends a timestamp to a shared log file. Run loader 5–10 times and check log: PluginB's timestamp always appears before PluginA's. This catches timing issues, module load order bugs, and race conditions in real execution.

behaviors.description: Circular plugin dependency is detected and neither plugin is loaded
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection is a graph algorithm (Unit-testable), but the requirement 'loads neither' is a behavior verification (Integration). Real failure: loader detects cycle but one or both plugins still partially load, run onLoad, or modify shared state. Only real plugin files can expose partial loading bugs. Mocking both as 'not loaded' is insufficient — need to verify neither's code actually executes.
behaviors.test_description: Create two real plugins: PluginX and PluginY with circular dependency in manifest.json (X → Y, Y → X). Each has an onLoad hook that writes a marker file. Run loader and expect a cycle error. Assert: (1) error is reported, (2) neither X's nor Y's marker file exists. Verify across multiple runs that no partial loading occurs.

behaviors.description: Plugin onLoad hook is invoked only after the plugin's entry module is imported and top-level code executes
behaviors.justification: Real failure: onLoad called before import completes (async race), or onLoad called despite import error. Unit test can mock import and verify logic order, but cannot catch Node.js async execution bugs or race conditions. Must use real module loading to expose timing issues. Entry module's top-level code must actually run and have observable side effects that onLoad can verify.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real plugin with manifest.json and entry.js. Entry.js has top-level code that writes to a completion marker file. onLoad hook reads that marker and fails if it doesn't exist. Run loader and assert: onLoad succeeds (proving entry code ran first). Run 10+ times to catch race conditions. Add async delays in entry.js to test async import completion scenarios.

behaviors.description: When one plugin's onLoad throws, loader continues initializing remaining non-dependent plugins
behaviors.justification: Unit test can mock three plugins and verify that C's onLoad is called while B's is skipped, but real error handling with real plugins may fail silently or differently. Real failure: loader stops after first error, or error unwinding breaks subsequent plugin loading. Only real plugin files, real onLoad hooks throwing real errors, and actual error propagation through the module system can expose these bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create three real plugins: PluginFail (onLoad throws error), PluginDependent (depends on PluginFail), PluginIndependent (no dependencies). Each has onLoad hook that writes marker file on success. Run loader, capture error logs. Assert: (1) PluginFail's error is logged, (2) PluginDependent's marker does NOT exist (skipped), (3) PluginIndependent's marker DOES exist (loaded despite error), (4) loader doesn't crash. Run multiple times with different error types (throw, async reject, etc.).
